Trabalho Práico - Extração de Conhecimento de Dados Biológicos

Diogo Barreira, Gonçalo Gomes, José Campos

2026-04-02

#1. Introdução

Os dados utilizados para o desenvolvimento do trabalho prático são provenientes do “The Cancer Genome Atlas” (TCGA), mais concretamente referentes ao carcinoma da tiróide (THCA) e foram consultados através do portal do National Cancer Institute. A escolha desta base de dados justifica-se pelo cancro da tiróide ser uma neoplasia endócrina muito comum (Wang et al., 2017; Du et al., 2021). Os autores acrescentam ainda que apesar de tipicamente os prognósticos serem bons, uma parte dos casos apresenta um comportamento muito agressivo. O TCGA-THCA permitiu que fossem identificados subtipos moleculares importantes, tais como perfis BRAF-like e RAS-like essenciais para a medicina de precisão (The Cancer Genome Atlas Research Network, 2014).

#2. Os Dados:

Os dados utilizados foram obtidos através do pacote TCGAbiolinks, que permite aceder diretamente ao repositório Genomic Data Commons (GDC).

Projeto: TCGA-THCA Tipo de dados: expressão génica (RNA-Seq) Formato: contagens de leitura por gene

Estes dados são relevantes porque permitem comparar a expressão génica entre tumores e tecidos normais, possibilitando a identificação de genes associados ao desenvolvimento do cancro.

#3. Carregamento e preparação dos dados:

if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
## Warning: package 'BiocManager' was built under R version 4.5.3
BiocManager::install(version = "3.22", ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
## Bioconductor version 3.22 (BiocManager 1.30.27), R 4.5.1 (2025-06-13 ucrt)
BiocManager::install(c("GenomicFeatures", "AnnotationDbi"), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
## Bioconductor version 3.22 (BiocManager 1.30.27), R 4.5.1 (2025-06-13 ucrt)
## Warning: package(s) not installed when version(s) same as or greater than current; use
##   `force = TRUE` to re-install: 'GenomicFeatures' 'AnnotationDbi'
BiocManager::install("TCGAbiolinks", ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
## Bioconductor version 3.22 (BiocManager 1.30.27), R 4.5.1 (2025-06-13 ucrt)
## Warning: package(s) not installed when version(s) same as or greater than current; use
##   `force = TRUE` to re-install: 'TCGAbiolinks'

Bibliotecas a serem utilizadas:

library(BiocManager)
library(TCGAbiolinks)
## Warning: package 'TCGAbiolinks' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(SummarizedExperiment)
## Warning: package 'SummarizedExperiment' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Loading required package: MatrixGenerics
## Warning: package 'MatrixGenerics' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Loading required package: matrixStats
## Warning: package 'matrixStats' was built under R version 4.5.3
## 
## Attaching package: 'MatrixGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     colAlls, colAnyNAs, colAnys, colAvgsPerRowSet, colCollapse,
##     colCounts, colCummaxs, colCummins, colCumprods, colCumsums,
##     colDiffs, colIQRDiffs, colIQRs, colLogSumExps, colMadDiffs,
##     colMads, colMaxs, colMeans2, colMedians, colMins, colOrderStats,
##     colProds, colQuantiles, colRanges, colRanks, colSdDiffs, colSds,
##     colSums2, colTabulates, colVarDiffs, colVars, colWeightedMads,
##     colWeightedMeans, colWeightedMedians, colWeightedSds,
##     colWeightedVars, rowAlls, rowAnyNAs, rowAnys, rowAvgsPerColSet,
##     rowCollapse, rowCounts, rowCummaxs, rowCummins, rowCumprods,
##     rowCumsums, rowDiffs, rowIQRDiffs, rowIQRs, rowLogSumExps,
##     rowMadDiffs, rowMads, rowMaxs, rowMeans2, rowMedians, rowMins,
##     rowOrderStats, rowProds, rowQuantiles, rowRanges, rowRanks,
##     rowSdDiffs, rowSds, rowSums2, rowTabulates, rowVarDiffs, rowVars,
##     rowWeightedMads, rowWeightedMeans, rowWeightedMedians,
##     rowWeightedSds, rowWeightedVars
## Loading required package: GenomicRanges
## Warning: package 'GenomicRanges' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Loading required package: stats4
## Loading required package: BiocGenerics
## Warning: package 'BiocGenerics' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Loading required package: generics
## 
## Attaching package: 'generics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff,
##     setequal, union
## 
## Attaching package: 'BiocGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind,
##     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find,
##     get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget,
##     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank,
##     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique,
##     unsplit, which.max, which.min
## Loading required package: S4Vectors
## Warning: package 'S4Vectors' was built under R version 4.5.2
## 
## Attaching package: 'S4Vectors'
## The following object is masked from 'package:utils':
## 
##     findMatches
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     expand.grid, I, unname
## Loading required package: IRanges
## Warning: package 'IRanges' was built under R version 4.5.2
## 
## Attaching package: 'IRanges'
## The following object is masked from 'package:grDevices':
## 
##     windows
## Loading required package: Seqinfo
## Warning: package 'Seqinfo' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Loading required package: Biobase
## Warning: package 'Biobase' was built under R version 4.5.3
## Welcome to Bioconductor
## 
##     Vignettes contain introductory material; view with
##     'browseVignettes()'. To cite Bioconductor, see
##     'citation("Biobase")', and for packages 'citation("pkgname")'.
## 
## Attaching package: 'Biobase'
## The following object is masked from 'package:MatrixGenerics':
## 
##     rowMedians
## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     anyMissing, rowMedians
query_thca <- GDCquery(
    project = "TCGA-THCA",
    data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling",
    data.type = "Gene Expression Quantification",
    workflow.type = "STAR - Counts"
)
## --------------------------------------
## o GDCquery: Searching in GDC database
## --------------------------------------
## Genome of reference: hg38
## --------------------------------------------
## oo Accessing GDC. This might take a while...
## --------------------------------------------
## ooo Project: TCGA-THCA
## --------------------
## oo Filtering results
## --------------------
## ooo By data.type
## ooo By workflow.type
## ----------------
## oo Checking data
## ----------------
## ooo Checking if there are duplicated cases
## ooo Checking if there are results for the query
## -------------------
## o Preparing output
## -------------------
GDCdownload(query_thca)
## Downloading data for project TCGA-THCA
## Of the 572 files for download 572 already exist.
## All samples have been already downloaded
data_thca <- GDCprepare(query_thca)

|=================================================== |98.95105% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |99.12587% ~0 s remaining |====================================================|100% ~0 s remaining       |====================================================|100%                      Completed after 34 s
## Starting to add information to samples
##  => Add clinical information to samples
##  => Adding TCGA molecular information from marker papers
##  => Information will have prefix 'paper_'
## thca subtype information from:doi:10.1016/j.cell.2014.09.050
## Available assays in SummarizedExperiment : 
##   => unstranded
##   => stranded_first
##   => stranded_second
##   => tpm_unstrand
##   => fpkm_unstrand
##   => fpkm_uq_unstrand
#Nesta etapa, pretendemos descarregar os dados de expressão de genes.
exp_matrix <- assay(data_thca)

#aqui extrai-se uma matriz de expressão onde as linhas vão representar os genes e as colunas representam as amostras.

3.1 Dimensões do dataset

cat("Dimensões antes da filtragem:", dim(exp_matrix), "\n")
## Dimensões antes da filtragem: 60660 572

4. Pré-processamento dos dados

4.1 Filtragem de genes pouco expressos

keep <- rowSums(exp_matrix > 10) >= 10
exp_filtered <- exp_matrix[keep, ]

#Aqui pretendemos remover os genes de baixa expressão.

##4.2 Dimensões após filtragem + distribuição dos grupos

cat("Dimensões após filtragem:", dim(exp_filtered), "\n")
## Dimensões após filtragem: 28300 572

4.3 Transformação logarítmica

exp_log <- log2(exp_filtered + 1)

#Transformamos os dados através de log2, irá estabilizar a variância e tornar os dados mais adequados para realizar uma análise estatística.

5. Análise exploratória e estatística descritiva

5.1 Estatísticas básicas

summary(exp_log)

##  Min.   : 0.000               Min.   : 0.000              
##  1st Qu.: 3.000               1st Qu.: 3.322              
##  Median : 6.524               Median : 6.919              
##  Mean   : 6.609               Mean   : 7.086              
##  3rd Qu.:10.347               3rd Qu.:10.933              
##  Max.   :21.453               Max.   :21.383              
##  TCGA-EL-A3T1-11A-11R-A22L-07 TCGA-DJ-A2PS-01A-11R-A18C-07
##  Min.   : 0.000               Min.   : 0.000              
##  1st Qu.: 3.322               1st Qu.: 3.000              
##  Median : 6.807               Median : 6.585              
##  Mean   : 6.911               Mean   : 6.717              
##  3rd Qu.:10.504               3rd Qu.:10.471              
##  Max.   :21.602               Max.   :21.039              
##  TCGA-EL-A3TB-11A-11R-A22L-07 TCGA-EL-A3TB-01A-11R-A22L-07
##  Min.   : 0.000               Min.   : 0.000              
##  1st Qu.: 3.459               1st Qu.: 3.170              
##  Median : 7.011               Median : 6.768              
##  Mean   : 7.110               Mean   : 6.884              
##  3rd Qu.:10.823               3rd Qu.:10.623              
##  Max.   :22.580               Max.   :20.991              
##  TCGA-BJ-A45C-01A-11R-A23W-07 TCGA-DJ-A3UX-01A-11R-A22L-07
##  Min.   : 0.000               Min.   : 0.000              
##  1st Qu.: 3.322               1st Qu.: 3.459              
##  Median : 6.807               Median : 6.943              
##  Mean   : 6.863               Mean   : 7.121              
##  3rd Qu.:10.563               3rd Qu.:10.772              
##  Max.   :21.813               Max.   :21.513              
##  TCGA-DJ-A2PX-01A-11R-A18C-07 TCGA-EL-A3ZS-01A-12R-A23N-07
##  Min.   : 0.000               Min.   : 0.000              
##  1st Qu.: 3.459               1st Qu.: 3.807              
##  Median : 7.109               Median : 7.180              
##  Mean   : 7.136               Mean   : 7.219              
##  3rd Qu.:10.836               3rd Qu.:10.664              
##  Max.   :21.456               Max.   :20.066
#recorrendo ao summary, conseguimos verificar os mínimos, máximos, média e quartis.

5.2 Visualização da distribuição

hist(exp_matrix, main="Distribuição original", col="lightblue")

hist(exp_log, main="Distribuição log2", col="lightgreen")

#De acordo com os gráficos, é possível inferir que a logaritmização dos dados reduz a assimetria da distribuição.

5.3 Boxplot das amostras

boxplot(exp_log, main="Boxplot das amostras", las=2)

#Este boxplot permite identificar diferenças entre a amostra e possíveis outliers.

5.4 Heatmap dos genes mais variáveis

library(pheatmap)
## Warning: package 'pheatmap' was built under R version 4.5.3
var_genes <- apply(exp_log, 1, var)
top_var_genes <- names(sort(var_genes, decreasing=TRUE))[1:50]

pheatmap(exp_log[top_var_genes, ])

#6. Definição dos grupos

group <- ifelse(substr(colnames(exp_log), 14, 15) == "01", "Tumor", "Normal")
table(group)
## group
## Normal  Tumor 
##     67    505

As amostras são classificadas como:

Tumor Normal

6.1 PCA das amostras

pca <- prcomp(t(exp_log), scale. = TRUE)
plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     col  = ifelse(group == "Tumor", "red", "blue"),
     pch  = 16,
     xlab = paste0("PC1 (", round(summary(pca)$importance[2,1]*100, 1), "%)"),
     ylab = paste0("PC2 (", round(summary(pca)$importance[2,2]*100, 1), "%)"),
     main = "PCA das amostras")
legend("topright", legend = c("Tumor", "Normal"), col = c("red", "blue"), pch = 16)

#7. Análise de expressão diferencial

p_values <- apply(exp_log, 1, function(gene) {
  t.test(gene ~ group)$p.value
}) #Realiza-se um teste t para cada gene para comparar os dois grupos.

p_adj <- p.adjust(p_values, method = "fdr")
diff_genes <- which(p_adj < 0.05) #A correção FDR é utilizada para controlar falsos positivos.

#7.1 Contagem de DEGs e tabela top genes

cat("Genes diferencialmente expressos (FDR < 0.05):", length(diff_genes), "\n")
## Genes diferencialmente expressos (FDR < 0.05): 18572

##7.2 Volcano plot

logFC <- rowMeans(exp_log[, group == "Tumor"]) -
         rowMeans(exp_log[, group == "Normal"])

plot(logFC, -log10(p_adj), pch=20, 
     main="Volcano Plot",
     xlab="Log Fold Change",
     ylab="-log10(p-value)")

#Este volcano plot foi utilizado para visualizar quais são os genes significativamente alterados.

##7.3Tabela top DEGs

top_degs <- data.frame(
  logFC = logFC[diff_genes],
  p_adj = p_adj[diff_genes]
)
head(top_degs[order(top_degs$p_adj), ], 20)
##                        logFC        p_adj
## ENSG00000137077.8  -6.104609 2.735625e-38
## ENSG00000143196.5  -4.890773 2.257070e-35
## ENSG00000039537.14 -3.751651 2.520640e-35
## ENSG00000196616.14 -4.750055 8.381511e-33
## ENSG00000130595.19 -3.234983 2.456111e-30
## ENSG00000231246.2  -2.699262 5.221089e-30
## ENSG00000101938.15 -4.425668 4.859036e-28
## ENSG00000126218.12 -2.593505 3.881213e-27
## ENSG00000063127.16 -1.830417 5.517752e-27
## ENSG00000147257.15 -3.781958 5.599652e-27
## ENSG00000151892.15 -3.238047 6.232971e-27
## ENSG00000179915.24 -3.640694 6.249644e-27
## ENSG00000205221.12 -4.383244 6.249644e-27
## ENSG00000230587.2  -2.701673 8.309230e-27
## ENSG00000070193.5  -4.233078 1.152366e-26
## ENSG00000138356.14 -3.745662 1.152366e-26
## ENSG00000111339.12 -2.798475 1.376473e-26
## ENSG00000127241.18 -3.224377 1.744490e-26
## ENSG00000168079.17 -5.128585 1.938720e-26
## ENSG00000180828.3  -2.037712 2.774685e-26

#8. Análise de enriquecimento funcional

genes <- rownames(exp_log)[diff_genes]

genes_clean <- sub("\\..*", "", genes)

head(genes_clean) # Limpeza dos Ids
## [1] "ENSG00000000005" "ENSG00000000419" "ENSG00000000457" "ENSG00000000971"
## [5] "ENSG00000001036" "ENSG00000001084"
library(clusterProfiler)
## Warning: package 'clusterProfiler' was built under R version 4.5.3
## 
## clusterProfiler v4.18.4 Learn more at https://yulab-smu.top/contribution-knowledge-mining/
## 
## Please cite:
## 
## S Xu, E Hu, Y Cai, Z Xie, X Luo, L Zhan, W Tang, Q Wang, B Liu, R Wang,
## W Xie, T Wu, L Xie, G Yu. Using clusterProfiler to characterize
## multiomics data. Nature Protocols. 2024, 19(11):3292-3320
## 
## Attaching package: 'clusterProfiler'
## The following object is masked from 'package:IRanges':
## 
##     slice
## The following object is masked from 'package:S4Vectors':
## 
##     rename
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
## Loading required package: AnnotationDbi
## Warning: package 'AnnotationDbi' was built under R version 4.5.2
## 
## Attaching package: 'AnnotationDbi'
## The following object is masked from 'package:clusterProfiler':
## 
##     select
## 
entrez_ids <- bitr(
  genes_clean,
  fromType = "ENSEMBL",
  toType = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db
)
## 'select()' returned 1:many mapping between keys and columns
## Warning in bitr(genes_clean, fromType = "ENSEMBL", toType = "ENTREZID", :
## 24.76% of input gene IDs are fail to map...
genes <- rownames(exp_log)[diff_genes]

ego <- enrichGO(
  gene = entrez_ids$ENTREZID,
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05
)

dotplot(ego, showCategory = 10)

#o "enrich" tem como finalidade averiguar os processos biológicos enriquecidos nos genes diferencialmente expressos.

Foi necessário converter os identificadores ENSEMBL para ENTREZID e remover sufixos de versão, uma vez que ferramentas de enriquecimento funcional requerem formatos específicos de identificação génica.

Aproximadamente 24.76% dos genes não foram convertidos para ENTREZ ID. Isto é um resultado esperado devido a limitações nas bases de dados e diferenças na anotação genética.
